name: get_orderbook
description: Retrieve and analyze the Stellar DEX orderbook for a trading pair. Returns raw bids/asks plus derived analytics including spread, mid price, liquidity depth, and order book imbalance. Useful for market making, arbitrage detection, and liquidity analysis.

selling_asset_code.description: Asset code being sold (e.g. XLM, USDC). Must be 1-12 alphanumeric characters.

selling_asset_issuer.description: Issuer account for selling asset. Omit for XLM native. Must be a valid Stellar account ID starting with G (56 characters).

buying_asset_code.description: Asset code being bought (e.g. XLM, USDC). Must be 1-12 alphanumeric characters.

buying_asset_issuer.description: Issuer account for buying asset. Omit for XLM native. Must be a valid Stellar account ID starting with G (56 characters).

limit.description: Number of price levels to return per side. Must be between 1 and 200. Default: 20. Use lower values (5-20) for quick market snapshots, higher values (50-200) for deep liquidity analysis.

depth_levels.description: Price percentage levels for depth analysis (e.g. [1, 2, 5] for 1%, 2%, 5% from mid price). Default: [1, 2, 5]. Used to compute bid/ask liquidity within specified percentage ranges of the mid price.

network.description: Override the configured network for this call. Default: uses configured network.

bids.description: Array of bid orders (buy orders), sorted by price descending (best bid first)

price.description: Price as a decimal string (7 decimal places)

amount.description: Amount available at this price level (7 decimal places)

asks.description: Array of ask orders (sell orders), sorted by price ascending (best ask first)

price.description: Price as a decimal string (7 decimal places)

amount.description: Amount available at this price level (7 decimal places)

empty_book.description: True if orderbook has no bids and no asks

analytics.description: Derived analytics computed from raw orderbook data. Null if orderbook is empty.

best_bid.description: Highest bid price (top of buy side). Null if no bids. Formula: bids[0].price

best_ask.description: Lowest ask price (top of sell side). Null if no asks. Formula: asks[0].price

mid_price.description: Mid-market price. Null if missing bid or ask. Formula: (best_bid + best_ask) / 2

spread.description: Absolute spread between best bid and ask. Null if missing bid or ask. Formula: best_ask - best_bid

spread_percentage.description: Spread as percentage of best ask. Null if missing bid or ask. Formula: (spread / best_ask) * 100

total_bid_liquidity.description: Sum of all bid amounts. Formula: sum(bids[].amount)

total_ask_liquidity.description: Sum of all ask amounts. Formula: sum(asks[].amount)

bid_depth_at_levels.description: Total bid liquidity within each configured percentage level below mid price. Keys are percentage levels (e.g. '1', '2', '5'), values are liquidity amounts.

ask_depth_at_levels.description: Total ask liquidity within each configured percentage level above mid price. Keys are percentage levels (e.g. '1', '2', '5'), values are liquidity amounts.

orderbook_imbalance.description: Orderbook imbalance ratio. Ranges from -1 (all asks) to +1 (all bids). 0 indicates balanced orderbook. Null if total volume is zero. Formula: (bid_volume - ask_volume) / (bid_volume + ask_volume)

weighted_avg_bid_price.description: Volume-weighted average bid price. Null if no bids. Formula: sum(bid.price * bid.amount) / sum(bid.amount)

weighted_avg_ask_price.description: Volume-weighted average ask price. Null if no asks. Formula: sum(ask.price * ask.amount) / sum(ask.amount)

errors.message: Asset code must be 1–12 alphanumeric characters
Non-native assets require an issuer account ID
Issuer must be a valid Stellar account ID (G...)

errors.trigger: Limit parameter out of valid range

errors.message: Request rate limited, please slow down

errors.trigger: Horizon API is unreachable or returning 503/504

examples.description: Get XLM/USDC orderbook with default settings
Get top 5 levels of USDC/XLM orderbook with custom depth analysis

use_cases: Market making: Monitor spread and liquidity to optimize quote placement
Arbitrage detection: Compare orderbooks across trading pairs to identify price discrepancies
Liquidity analysis: Assess market depth at various price levels for large order execution
Trading strategy: Use orderbook imbalance as a signal for directional bias
Risk management: Monitor spread percentage and depth to assess market quality

performance_notes.caching: No caching - orderbook data is real-time by nature
